   166→
   167→    const result = parseCodexTranscript(filePath)
   168→    expect(result.tool_events).toHaveLength(2)
   169→    expect(result.tool_events[0]!.summary).toContain('readFile input=')
   170→    expect(result.tool_events[1]!.summary).toContain('readFile output=')
   171→  })
   172→
   173→  it('parses custom_tool_call and custom_tool_call_output', () => {

   197→    const result = parseCodexTranscript(filePath)

   202→
   203→  it('deduplicates identical messages', () => {

   415→describe('parseGeminiTranscript', () => {

   540→    writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify([{ role: 'user', text: 'Hi', timestamp: '2024-06-01T10:00:00Z' }]))
   541→
   542→    const result = parseGeminiTranscript(filePath)

   621→    expect(result.messages[0]!.text).toBe('auto-detected')

   157→describe('transcriptMatchesRepo', () => {

   163→  afterEach(() => {

   202→  })
   203→})

   234→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   235→// discoverSourceFiles — integration test with temp directories
   236→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   237→
   238→describe('discoverSourceFiles', () => {
   239→  let tmpDir: string
   240→  let codexRoot: string
   241→  let claudeRoot: string
   242→  let geminiRoot: string
   243→  let mockRoots: Record<string, string>
   244→
   245→  beforeEach(() => {
   246→    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'discover-'))
   247→    codexRoot = join(tmpDir, '.codex')
   248→    claudeRoot = join(tmpDir, '.claude')
   249→    geminiRoot = join(tmpDir, '.gemini')
   250→    mockRoots = { codex: codexRoot, claude: claudeRoot, gemini: geminiRoot }
   251→  })
   252→
   253→  afterEach(() => {
   254→    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
   255→  })
   256→
   257→  it('discovers codex session files', () => {
   258→    const sessionsDir = join(codexRoot, 'sessions', 'abc')
   259→    mkdirSync(sessionsDir, { recursive: true })
   260→    writeFileSync(join(sessionsDir, 'session.jsonl'), '{"type":"test"}\n')
   261→
   262→    const results = discoverSourceFiles(new Set(['codex']), mockRoots)
   263→    expect(results.length).toBe(1)
   264→    expect(results[0]![0]).toBe('codex')
   265→    expect(results[0]![1]).toContain('session.jsonl')
   266→  })
   267→
   268→  it('discovers claude project files', () => {
   269→    const projectDir = join(claudeRoot, 'projects', 'a--my-project')
   270→    mkdirSync(projectDir, { recursive: true })
   271→    writeFileSync(join(projectDir, 'chat.jsonl'), '{"msg":"hi"}\n')
   272→
   273→    const results = discoverSourceFiles(new Set(['claude']), mockRoots)
   274→    expect(results.length).toBe(1)
   275→    expect(results[0]![0]).toBe('claude')
   276→    expect(results[0]![1]).toContain('chat.jsonl')
   277→  })
   278→
   279→  it('discovers gemini history files', () => {
   280→    const historyDir = join(geminiRoot, 'history', 'my-app')
   281→    mkdirSync(historyDir, { recursive: true })
   282→    writeFileSync(join(historyDir, 'session.json'), '{}')
   283→
   284→    const results = discoverSourceFiles(new Set(['gemini']), mockRoots)
   285→    expect(results.length).toBe(1)
   286→    expect(results[0]![0]).toBe('gemini')
   287→    expect(results[0]![1]).toContain('session.json')
   288→  })
   289→
   290→  it('skips clients whose root does not exist', () => {
   291→    // codexRoot does not exist on disk — nothing was mkdirSync'd
   292→    const results = discoverSourceFiles(new Set(['codex']), mockRoots)
   293→    expect(results).toEqual([])
   294→  })
   295→
   296→  it('sorts results by mtime descending', () => {
   297→    const sessionsDir = join(codexRoot, 'sessions')
   298→    mkdirSync(sessionsDir, { recursive: true })
   299→
   300→    const older = join(sessionsDir, 'older.jsonl')
   301→    const newer = join(sessionsDir, 'newer.jsonl')
   302→    writeFileSync(older, '{"a":1}\n')
   303→    writeFileSync(newer, '{"b":2}\n')
   304→
   305→    // Set explicit mtimes: older = 1000s ago, newer = 500s ago
   306→    const now = new Date()
   307→    const olderTime = new Date(now.getTime() - 1000_000)
   308→    const newerTime = new Date(now.getTime() - 500_000)
   309→    utimesSync(older, olderTime, olderTime)
   310→    utimesSync(newer, newerTime, newerTime)
   311→
   312→    const results = discoverSourceFiles(new Set(['codex']), mockRoots)
   313→    expect(results.length).toBe(2)
   314→    // Newer file should come first
   315→    expect(results[0]![1]).toContain('newer.jsonl')
   316→    expect(results[1]![1]).toContain('older.jsonl')
   317→  })
   318→
   319→  it('deduplicates files by resolved lowercase path', () => {
   320→    const historyDir = join(geminiRoot, 'history', 'proj')
   321→    mkdirSync(historyDir, { recursive: true })
   322→    writeFileSync(join(historyDir, 'data.json'), '{}')
   323→
   324→    const results = discoverSourceFiles(new Set(['gemini']), mockRoots)
   325→    // history/**/*.json matches data.json; should appear only once
   326→    expect(results.length).toBe(1)
   327→  })
   328→
   329→  it('discovers gemini checkpoint files', () => {
   330→    const checkpointDir = join(geminiRoot, 'tmp', 'workspace1', 'checkpoints', 'sub')
   331→    mkdirSync(checkpointDir, { recursive: true })
   332→    writeFileSync(join(checkpointDir, 'checkpoint.jsonl'), '{"c":3}\n')
   333→
   334→    const results = discoverSourceFiles(new Set(['gemini']), mockRoots)
   335→    expect(results.length).toBe(1)
   336→    expect(results[0]![0]).toBe('gemini')
   337→    expect(results[0]![1]).toContain('checkpoint.jsonl')
   338→  })
   339→})

   121→
   122→  it('writes no-tool-events placeholder when tool_events empty', () => {
   123→    const filePath = join(tmpDir, 'no-tools.md')
   124→    const parsed = makeParsed({ tool_events: [] })
   125→    const manifest = makeManifest()
   126→    writeCleanMarkdown(parsed, manifest, filePath)
   127→
   128→    const content = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')
   129→    expect(content).toContain('No tool events were extracted.')
   130→  })
   131→})
   132→
   133→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   134→// preserveExistingImportTimestamp
   135→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   136→
   137→describe('preserveExistingImportTimestamp', () => {
   138→  it('returns payload when existing is null', () => {
   139→    const payload = makeManifest()
   140→    expect(preserveExistingImportTimestamp(null, payload)).toBe(payload)
   141→  })
   142→
   143→  it('preserves imported_at when signatures match', () => {
   144→    const existing = { ...makeManifest(), imported_at: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z' } as Record<string, unknown>
   145→    const payload = makeManifest({ imported_at: '2024-06-15T13:00:00Z' })
   146→    const result = preserveExistingImportTimestamp(existing, payload)
   147→    expect(result.imported_at).toBe('2024-01-01T00:00:00Z')
   148→  })
   149→
   150→  it('returns new payload when signatures differ', () => {
   151→    const existing = { ...makeManifest({ title: 'old' }), imported_at: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z' } as Record<string, unknown>
   152→    const payload = makeManifest({ title: 'new', imported_at: '2024-06-15T13:00:00Z' })
   153→    const result = preserveExistingImportTimestamp(existing, payload)
   154→    expect(result.imported_at).toBe('2024-06-15T13:00:00Z')
   155→  })
   156→})
   157→
   158→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   159→// manifestSignature
   160→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   161→
   162→describe('manifestSignature', () => {
   163→  it('excludes imported_at', () => {
   164→    const sig = manifestSignature({ a: 1, imported_at: 'x', b: 2 })
   165→    expect(sig).toEqual({ a: 1, b: 2 })
   166→  })
   167→})
   168→
   169→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   170→// manifestChanged
   171→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→
   173→describe('manifestChanged', () => {
   174→  let tmpDir: string
   175→
   176→  beforeEach(() => { tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'manifest-')) })
   177→  afterEach(() => { rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true }) })
   178→
   179→  it('returns true for non-existent file', () => {
   180→    expect(manifestChanged(join(tmpDir, 'missing.json'), { a: 1 })).toBe(true)
   181→  })
   182→
   183→  it('returns false when content matches', () => {
   184→    const filePath = join(tmpDir, 'test.json')
   185→    writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify({ a: 1 }))
   186→    expect(manifestChanged(filePath, { a: 1 })).toBe(false)
   187→  })
   188→
   189→  it('returns true when content differs', () => {
   190→    const filePath = join(tmpDir, 'test.json')
   191→    writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify({ a: 1 }))
   192→    expect(manifestChanged(filePath, { a: 2 })).toBe(true)
   193→  })
   194→})
   195→
   196→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   197→// writeJson / appendJsonl
   198→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   199→
   200→describe('writeJson', () => {
   201→  let tmpDir: string
   202→
   203→  beforeEach(() => { tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'json-')) })
   204→  afterEach(() => { rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true }) })
   205→
   206→  it('writes formatted JSON with trailing newline', () => {
   207→    const filePath = join(tmpDir, 'test.json')
   208→    writeJson(filePath, { a: 1, b: 'hello' })
   209→    const content = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')
   210→    expect(content).toBe('{\n  "a": 1,\n  "b": "hello"\n}\n')
   211→  })
   212→})
   213→
   214→describe('appendJsonl', () => {
   215→  let tmpDir: string
   216→
   217→  beforeEach(() => { tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'jsonl-')) })
   218→  afterEach(() => { rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true }) })
   219→
   220→  it('appends single JSON line', () => {
   221→    const filePath = join(tmpDir, 'test.jsonl')
   222→    writeFileSync(filePath, '')
   223→    appendJsonl(filePath, { a: 1 })
   224→    appendJsonl(filePath, { b: 2 })
   225→    const lines = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\n')
   226→    expect(lines).toHaveLength(2)
   227→    expect(JSON.parse(lines[0]!)).toEqual({ a: 1 })
   228→    expect(JSON.parse(lines[1]!)).toEqual({ b: 2 })
   229→  })
   230→})
   231→
   232→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   233→// copyFile
   234→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   235→
   236→describe('copyFile', () => {
   237→  let tmpDir: string
   238→
   239→  beforeEach(() => { tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'copy-')) })
   240→  afterEach(() => { rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true }) })
   241→
   242→  it('copies file to destination', () => {
   243→    const src = join(tmpDir, 'src.txt')
   244→    const dst = join(tmpDir, 'dst.txt')
   245→    writeFileSync(src, 'hello')
   246→    copyFile(src, dst)
   247→    expect(readFileSync(dst, 'utf-8')).toBe('hello')
   248→  })
   249→
   250→  it('skips when source equals destination', () => {
   251→    const src = join(tmpDir, 'same.txt')
   252→    writeFileSync(src, 'hello')
   253→    copyFile(src, src)
   254→    expect(readFileSync(src, 'utf-8')).toBe('hello')
   255→  })
   256→})
   257→
   258→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   259→// importTranscript (end-to-end)
   260→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   261→
   262→describe('importTranscript', () => {
   263→  let tmpDir: string
   264→  let repoRoot: string
   265→  let globalRoot: string
   266→
   267→  beforeEach(() => {
   268→    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'import-'))
   269→    repoRoot = join(tmpDir, 'repo')
   270→    globalRoot = join(tmpDir, 'global')
   271→  })
   272→  afterEach(() => {
   273→    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
   274→  })
   275→
   276→  it('imports a transcript and creates all expected files', async () => {
   277→    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'test.jsonl')
   278→    writeFileSync(sourceFile, '{"type":"session_meta","payload":{"id":"s1"}}\n')
   279→
   280→    const parsed = makeParsed({ source_path: sourceFile })
   281→    const result = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'my-project', 'none', true)
   282→
   283→    expect(result.client).toBe('codex')
   284→    expect(result.project).toBe('my-project')
   285→    expect(result.message_count).toBe(2)
   286→    expect(result.tool_event_count).toBe(1)
   287→    expect(result.cleaning_mode).toBe('deterministic-code')
   288→    expect(result.repo_mirror_enabled).toBe(true)
   289→
   290→    // Verify files exist
   291→    expect(existsSync(join(repoRoot, result.repo_raw_path))).toBe(true)
   292→    expect(existsSync(join(repoRoot, result.repo_clean_path))).toBe(true)
   293→    expect(existsSync(join(repoRoot, result.repo_manifest_path))).toBe(true)
   294→  })
   295→
   296→  it('imports without repo mirror', async () => {
   297→    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'test2.jsonl')
   298→    writeFileSync(sourceFile, '{"type":"test"}\n')
   299→
   300→    const parsed = makeParsed({ source_path: sourceFile })
   301→    const result = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'my-project', 'none', false)
   302→
   303→    expect(result.repo_raw_path).toBe('')
   304→    expect(result.repo_clean_path).toBe('')
   305→    expect(result.repo_manifest_path).toBe('')
   306→    expect(result.repo_mirror_enabled).toBe(false)
   307→  })
   308→
   309→  it('preserves imported_at on idempotent re-import', async () => {
   310→    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'test3.jsonl')
   311→    writeFileSync(sourceFile, '{"type":"test"}\n')
   312→
   313→    const parsed = makeParsed({ source_path: sourceFile })
   314→    const first = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'my-project', 'none')
   315→    const firstImportedAt = first.imported_at
   316→
   317→    // Re-import (manifest exists, signature unchanged)
   318→    const second = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'my-project', 'none')
   319→    expect(second.imported_at).toBe(firstImportedAt)
   320→  })
   321→})
   322→

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, existsSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→import initSqlJs from 'sql.js'
     6→
     7→import { parseCodexTranscript } from '../../src/transcript/parse.js'
     8→import { importTranscript, transcriptHasContent } from '../../src/transcript/import.js'
     9→import { inferProjectSlug } from '../../src/transcript/discover.js'
    10→
    11→describe('integration: parse → import → verify', () => {
    12→  let tmpDir: string
    13→  let repoRoot: string
    14→  let globalRoot: string
    15→
    16→  beforeEach(() => {
    17→    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'integration-'))
    18→    repoRoot = join(tmpDir, 'repo')
    19→    globalRoot = join(tmpDir, 'global')
    20→  })
    21→  afterEach(() => {
    22→    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    23→  })
    24→
    25→  it('end-to-end: create JSONL → parse → import → verify files', async () => {
    26→    // 1. Create a realistic Codex JSONL transcript
    27→    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'test-session.jsonl')
    28→    const records = [
    29→      { type: 'session_meta', payload: { id: 'sess-001', title: 'Fix bug', cwd: '/home/user/myproject', git: { branch: 'main' }, timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:00Z' } },
    30→      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:01Z', payload: { type: 'message', role: 'user', content: [{ type: 'input_text', text: 'Fix the login bug' }] } },
    31→      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:02Z', payload: { type: 'message', role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'I will fix the login bug for you.' }] } },
    32→      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:03Z', payload: { type: 'function_call', name: 'read_file', arguments: { path: 'login.ts' } } },
    33→      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:04Z', payload: { type: 'function_call_output', name: 'read_file', output: 'file contents here' } },
    34→    ]
    35→    writeFileSync(sourceFile, records.map(r => JSON.stringify(r)).join('\n') + '\n')
    36→
    37→    // 2. Parse
    38→    const parsed = parseCodexTranscript(sourceFile)
    39→    expect(parsed.client).toBe('codex')
    40→    expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('sess-001')
    41→    expect(parsed.title).toBe('Fix bug')
    42→    expect(parsed.cwd).toBe('/home/user/myproject')
    43→    expect(parsed.branch).toBe('main')
    44→    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)
    45→    expect(parsed.tool_events).toHaveLength(2)
    46→    expect(transcriptHasContent(parsed)).toBe(true)
    47→
    48→    // 3. Infer project slug
    49→    const slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)
    50→    expect(slug).toBe('myproject')
    51→
    52→    // 4. Import
    53→    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, slug, 'none', true)
    54→
    55→    // 5. Verify manifest fields
    56→    expect(manifest.client).toBe('codex')
    57→    expect(manifest.project).toBe('myproject')
    58→    expect(manifest.session_id).toBe('sess-001')
    59→    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)
    60→    expect(manifest.tool_event_count).toBe(2)
    61→    expect(manifest.cleaning_mode).toBe('deterministic-code')
    62→    expect(manifest.repo_mirror_enabled).toBe(true)
    63→
    64→    // 6. Verify raw file copied to repo + global
    65→    expect(existsSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_raw_path))).toBe(true)
    66→
    67→    // 7. Verify clean markdown has correct frontmatter
    68→    const cleanContent = readFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_clean_path), 'utf-8')
    69→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('---')
    70→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('client: codex')
    71→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('project: myproject')
    72→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Messages')
    73→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('### 1. user')
    74→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('Fix the login bug')
    75→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('### 2. assistant')
    76→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('I will fix the login bug for you.')
    77→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Tool Events')
    78→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('read_file')
    79→
    80→    // 8. Verify manifest JSON written
    81→    const manifestContent = JSON.parse(
    82→      readFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_manifest_path), 'utf-8'),
    83→    )
    84→    expect(manifestContent.client).toBe('codex')
    85→    expect(manifestContent.project).toBe('myproject')
    86→
    87→    // 9. Verify SQLite index updated
    88→    const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')
    89→    expect(existsSync(dbPath)).toBe(true)
    90→    const SQL = await initSqlJs()
    91→    const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))
    92→    const rows = db.exec('SELECT client, project, session_id FROM transcripts')
    93→    expect(rows).toHaveLength(1)
    94→    expect(rows[0]!.values).toEqual([['codex', 'myproject', 'sess-001']])
    95→    db.close()
    96→
    97→    // 10. Verify event log written
    98→    const eventLogPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'sessions.jsonl')
    99→    expect(existsSync(eventLogPath)).toBe(true)
   100→    const eventLines = readFileSync(eventLogPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\n')
   101→    expect(eventLines).toHaveLength(1)
   102→    const event = JSON.parse(eventLines[0]!)
   103→    expect(event.client).toBe('codex')
   104→  })
   105→
   106→  it('idempotent re-import preserves imported_at', async () => {
   107→    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'idem.jsonl')
   108→    writeFileSync(sourceFile, '{"type":"session_meta","payload":{"id":"s1","title":"test"}}\n')
   109→
   110→    const parsed = parseCodexTranscript(sourceFile)
   111→    const first = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'proj', 'none')
   112→    const second = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'proj', 'none')
   113→
   114→    expect(second.imported_at).toBe(first.imported_at)
   115→  })
   116→
   117→  it('Claude transcript end-to-end', async () => {
   118→    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'claude-session.jsonl')
   119→    const records = [
   120→      { type: 'user', sessionId: 'cs1', cwd: '/proj', gitBranch: 'dev', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:00Z', message: { role: 'user', content: 'Hello Claude' } },
   121→      { type: 'assistant', sessionId: 'cs1', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:01Z', message: { role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Hello!' }] } },
   122→    ]
   123→    writeFileSync(sourceFile, records.map(r => JSON.stringify(r)).join('\n') + '\n')
   124→
   125→    const { parseClaudeTranscript } = await import('../../src/transcript/parse.js')
   126→    const parsed = parseClaudeTranscript(sourceFile)
   127→    expect(parsed.client).toBe('claude')
   128→    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)
   129→
   130→    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'test-proj', 'none')
   131→    expect(manifest.client).toBe('claude')
   132→    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)
   133→  })
   134→})
   135→

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Mock homedir so alertsPath() / failureStatePath() point to temp directory
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→let tmpDir: string
    11→
    12→vi.mock('node:os', async () => {
    13→  const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('node:os')>('node:os')
    14→  return {
    15→    ...actual,
    16→    homedir: () => tmpDir,
    17→  }
    18→})
    19→
    20→import {
    21→  readAlerts,
    22→  writeAlert,
    23→  writeAlertWithThreshold,
    24→  resetFailureCount,
    25→  clearAlerts,
    26→  formatAlertsForDisplay,
    27→  alertsPath,
    28→  MAX_ALERTS,
    29→  FAILURE_THRESHOLD,
    30→} from '../../src/heartbeat/alert.js'
    31→
    32→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    33→// Setup / Teardown
    34→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    35→
    36→beforeEach(() => {
    37→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'alert-test-'))
    38→})
    39→
    40→afterEach(() => {
    41→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    42→})
    43→
    44→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→// alertsPath
    46→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    47→
    48→describe('alertsPath', () => {
    49→  it('returns a path inside <home>/.memorytree/', () => {
    50→    const path = alertsPath()
    51→    expect(path).toContain('.memorytree')
    52→    expect(path).toContain('alerts.json')
    53→    expect(path.startsWith(tmpDir)).toBe(true)
    54→  })
    55→})
    56→
    57→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    58→// readAlerts
    59→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→
    61→describe('readAlerts', () => {
    62→  it('returns empty array when file does not exist', () => {
    63→    expect(readAlerts()).toEqual([])
    64→  })
    65→})
    66→
    67→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    68→// writeAlert
    69→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    70→
    71→describe('writeAlert', () => {
    72→  it('creates a new alert', () => {
    73→    writeAlert('my-project', 'push_failed', 'Push timed out')
    74→
    75→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    76→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    77→    expect(alerts[0]!.project).toBe('my-project')
    78→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('push_failed')
    79→    expect(alerts[0]!.message).toBe('Push timed out')
    80→    expect(alerts[0]!.count).toBe(1)
    81→    expect(alerts[0]!.timestamp).toBeTruthy()
    82→  })
    83→
    84→  it('deduplicates: updates count for same project+type', () => {
    85→    writeAlert('proj', 'push_failed', 'first error')
    86→    writeAlert('proj', 'push_failed', 'second error')
    87→
    88→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    89→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    90→    expect(alerts[0]!.count).toBe(2)
    91→    expect(alerts[0]!.message).toBe('second error')
    92→  })
    93→
    94→  it('does not merge alerts with different types', () => {
    95→    writeAlert('proj', 'push_failed', 'msg1')
    96→    writeAlert('proj', 'no_remote', 'msg2')
    97→
    98→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    99→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(2)
   100→  })
   101→
   102→  it('does not merge alerts with different projects', () => {
   103→    writeAlert('proj-a', 'push_failed', 'msg1')
   104→    writeAlert('proj-b', 'push_failed', 'msg2')
   105→
   106→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   107→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(2)
   108→  })
   109→
   110→  it('caps alerts at MAX_ALERTS', () => {
   111→    // Write MAX_ALERTS + 10 unique alerts
   112→    for (let i = 0; i < MAX_ALERTS + 10; i++) {
   113→      writeAlert(`proj-${i}`, 'push_failed', `msg-${i}`)
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   117→    expect(alerts.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(MAX_ALERTS)
   118→  })
   119→})
   120→
   121→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   122→// writeAlertWithThreshold
   123→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   124→
   125→describe('writeAlertWithThreshold', () => {
   126→  it('does not write alert until threshold is reached', () => {
   127→    // Write FAILURE_THRESHOLD - 1 times (below threshold)
   128→    for (let i = 0; i < FAILURE_THRESHOLD - 1; i++) {
   129→      writeAlertWithThreshold('proj', 'push_failed', 'msg')
   130→    }
   131→    expect(readAlerts()).toHaveLength(0)
   132→
   133→    // One more call reaches the threshold
   134→    writeAlertWithThreshold('proj', 'push_failed', 'msg')
   135→    expect(readAlerts()).toHaveLength(1)
   136→  })
   137→
   138→  it('writes on every subsequent call after threshold', () => {
   139→    // Reach threshold
   140→    for (let i = 0; i < FAILURE_THRESHOLD; i++) {
   141→      writeAlertWithThreshold('proj', 'push_failed', 'msg')
   142→    }
   143→    expect(readAlerts()).toHaveLength(1)
   144→
   145→    // One more increments beyond threshold, still writes (updates count)
   146→    writeAlertWithThreshold('proj', 'push_failed', 'msg again')
   147→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   148→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   149→    // Count should reflect the dedup in writeAlert
   150→    expect(alerts[0]!.count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2)
   151→  })
   152→})
   153→
   154→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   155→// resetFailureCount
   156→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   157→
   158→describe('resetFailureCount', () => {
   159→  it('resets the failure counter so threshold check restarts', () => {
   160→    // Accumulate failures below threshold
   161→    for (let i = 0; i < FAILURE_THRESHOLD - 1; i++) {
   162→      writeAlertWithThreshold('proj', 'push_failed', 'msg')
   163→    }
   164→
   165→    resetFailureCount('proj', 'push_failed')
   166→
   167→    // After reset, need FAILURE_THRESHOLD calls again to write
   168→    for (let i = 0; i < FAILURE_THRESHOLD - 1; i++) {
   169→      writeAlertWithThreshold('proj', 'push_failed', 'msg')
   170→    }
   171→    expect(readAlerts()).toHaveLength(0)
   172→  })
   173→
   174→  it('does nothing if key does not exist', () => {
   175→    // Should not throw
   176→    expect(() => resetFailureCount('nonexistent', 'push_failed')).not.toThrow()
   177→  })
   178→})
   179→
   180→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   181→// clearAlerts
   182→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   183→
   184→describe('clearAlerts', () => {
   185→  it('removes all alerts', () => {
   186→    writeAlert('proj', 'push_failed', 'msg')
   187→    expect(readAlerts()).toHaveLength(1)
   188→
   189→    clearAlerts()
   190→    expect(readAlerts()).toEqual([])
   191→  })
   192→
   193→  it('does nothing if no alerts file exists', () => {
   194→    expect(() => clearAlerts()).not.toThrow()
   195→  })
   196→})
   197→
   198→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   199→// formatAlertsForDisplay
   200→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   201→
   202→describe('formatAlertsForDisplay', () => {
   203→  it('returns empty string for empty array', () => {
   204→    expect(formatAlertsForDisplay([])).toBe('')
   205→  })
   206→
   207→  it('formats a single alert', () => {
   208→    const result = formatAlertsForDisplay([
   209→      { type: 'push_failed', project: 'my-proj', message: 'Push timed out', count: 1 },
   210→    ])
   211→    expect(result).toContain('[push_failed]')
   212→    expect(result).toContain('my-proj')
   213→    expect(result).toContain('Push timed out')
   214→    // count=1 should NOT show suffix
   215→    expect(result).not.toContain('(x1)')
   216→  })
   217→
   218→  it('shows count suffix when count > 1', () => {
   219→    const result = formatAlertsForDisplay([
   220→      { type: 'push_failed', project: 'proj', message: 'err', count: 5 },
   221→    ])
   222→    expect(result).toContain('(x5)')
   223→  })
   224→
   225→  it('formats multiple alerts on separate lines', () => {
   226→    const result = formatAlertsForDisplay([
   227→      { type: 'push_failed', project: 'proj-a', message: 'err1', count: 1 },
   228→      { type: 'no_remote', project: 'proj-b', message: 'err2', count: 3 },
   229→    ])
   230→    const lines = result.split('\n')
   231→    expect(lines).toHaveLength(2)
   232→    expect(lines[0]).toContain('proj-a')
   233→    expect(lines[1]).toContain('proj-b')
   234→    expect(lines[1]).toContain('(x3)')
   235→  })
   236→})
   237→

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, rmSync, mkdirSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Mock homedir so all path helpers point to a temp directory
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→let tmpDir: string
    11→
    12→vi.mock('node:os', async () => {
    13→  const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('node:os')>('node:os')
    14→  return {
    15→    ...actual,
    16→    homedir: () => tmpDir,
    17→  }
    18→})
    19→
    20→// Import after mock setup so configPath() / memorytreeRoot() use the mock
    21→import {
    22→  loadConfig,
    23→  saveConfig,

    25→  registerProject,
    26→  configPath,

    29→
    30→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    37→
    38→afterEach(() => {

    42→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    44→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→
    46→describe('configPath / memorytreeRoot', () => {
    47→  it('memorytreeRoot returns <home>/.memorytree', () => {

    63→
    64→describe('loadConfig', () => {
    65→  it('returns defaults when config file does not exist', () => {
    66→    const cfg = loadConfig()
    67→    expect(cfg.heartbeat_interval).toBe('5m')

    69→    expect(cfg.log_level).toBe('info')
    70→    expect(cfg.watch_dirs).toEqual([])
    71→    expect(cfg.projects).toEqual([])
    72→  })
    73→
    74→  it('parses valid TOML file', () => {
    75→    const path = configPath()
    76→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })
    77→    writeFileSync(
    78→      path,
    79→      [
    80→        'heartbeat_interval = "10m"',
    81→        'auto_push = false',
    82→        'log_level = "debug"',
    83→        'watch_dirs = ["/home/user/repos"]',
    84→        '',
    85→        '[[projects]]',
    86→        'path = "/home/user/project-a"',

    91→
    92→    const cfg = loadConfig()

    94→    expect(cfg.auto_push).toBe(false)

    98→    expect(cfg.projects[0]!.path).toBe('/home/user/project-a')

   101→
   102→  it('returns defaults for invalid TOML', () => {

   112→  it('uses defaults for missing or invalid fields', () => {

   127→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   128→// saveConfig
   129→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   130→
   131→describe('saveConfig', () => {
   132→  it('roundtrip: save then load yields equivalent config', () => {
   133→    const original = {
   134→      heartbeat_interval: '10m',
   135→      auto_push: false,
   136→      log_level: 'debug',
   137→      watch_dirs: ['/repos/a', '/repos/b'],
   138→      projects: [
   139→        { path: '/home/user/project-a', name: 'project-a' },
   140→        { path: '/home/user/project-b', name: 'project-b' },

   144→    saveConfig(original)

   147→    expect(loaded.heartbeat_interval).toBe(original.heartbeat_interval)

   149→    expect(loaded.log_level).toBe(original.log_level)

   156→  it('serializes empty projects list', () => {

   165→    saveConfig(cfg)
   166→
   167→    const text = readFileSync(configPath(), 'utf-8')
   168→    expect(text).toContain('heartbeat_interval = "5m"')
   169→    expect(text).toContain('auto_push = true')
   170→    expect(text).not.toContain('[[projects]]')

   172→})
   173→
   174→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   175→// intervalToSeconds
   176→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   177→
   178→describe('intervalToSeconds', () => {
   179→  it('parses minutes: 5m -> 300', () => {
   180→    expect(intervalToSeconds('5m')).toBe(300)
   181→  })
   182→
   183→  it('parses hours: 1h -> 3600', () => {
   184→    expect(intervalToSeconds('1h')).toBe(3600)
   185→  })
   186→
   187→  it('parses seconds: 30s -> 30', () => {
   188→    expect(intervalToSeconds('30s')).toBe(30)

   190→
   191→  it('returns default (300) for invalid input', () => {
   192→    expect(intervalToSeconds('invalid')).toBe(300)

   194→

   196→    expect(intervalToSeconds('0m')).toBe(300)

   198→
   199→  it('handles leading/trailing whitespace', () => {
   200→    expect(intervalToSeconds('  10m  ')).toBe(600)
   201→  })
   202→
   203→  it('is case-insensitive', () => {
   204→    expect(intervalToSeconds('2H')).toBe(7200)
   205→  })
   206→})
   207→
   208→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   209→// registerProject
   210→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   211→
   212→describe('registerProject', () => {
   213→  it('adds a new project to an empty list', () => {
   214→    const cfg = {
   215→      heartbeat_interval: '5m',

   229→  it('skips duplicate project (by resolved path)', () => {

   238→    const updated1 = registerProject(cfg, tmpDir)
   239→    const updated2 = registerProject(updated1, tmpDir)

   242→
   243→  it('does not mutate the original config', () => {

   248→      watch_dirs: [],

   252→    const updated = registerProject(cfg, tmpDir)

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, rmSync, readdirSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Mock homedir so log files go to a temp directory
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→let tmpDir: string
    11→
    12→vi.mock('node:os', async () => {
    13→  const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('node:os')>('node:os')
    14→  return {
    15→    ...actual,
    16→    homedir: () => tmpDir,
    17→  }
    18→})
    19→
    20→import {
    21→  setupLogging,
    22→  getLogger,
    23→  _resetLogger,
    24→} from '../../src/heartbeat/log.js'
    25→
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→// Setup / Teardown
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→
    30→beforeEach(() => {
    31→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'log-test-'))
    32→  _resetLogger()
    33→})
    34→
    35→afterEach(() => {
    36→  _resetLogger()
    37→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    38→})
    39→
    40→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→// Helpers
    42→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→
    44→function findLogFile(): string | undefined {
    45→  const logsDir = join(tmpDir, '.memorytree', 'logs')
    46→  try {
    47→    const files = readdirSync(logsDir)
    48→    return files.find(f => f.startsWith('heartbeat-') && f.endsWith('.log'))
    49→  } catch {
    50→    return undefined
    51→  }
    52→}
    53→
    54→function readLogContents(): string {
    55→  const logsDir = join(tmpDir, '.memorytree', 'logs')
    56→  const file = findLogFile()
    57→  if (!file) return ''
    58→  return readFileSync(join(logsDir, file), 'utf-8')
    59→}
    60→
    61→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→// setupLogging
    63→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→
    65→describe('setupLogging', () => {
    66→  it('returns a Logger with all required methods', () => {
    67→    const logger = setupLogging('info')
    68→    expect(typeof logger.debug).toBe('function')
    69→    expect(typeof logger.info).toBe('function')
    70→    expect(typeof logger.warn).toBe('function')
    71→    expect(typeof logger.error).toBe('function')
    72→    expect(typeof logger.exception).toBe('function')
    73→  })
    74→
    75→  it('writes log messages to a file', () => {
    76→    const logger = setupLogging('info')
    77→    logger.info('test message from setupLogging')
    78→
    79→    const contents = readLogContents()
    80→    expect(contents).toContain('test message from setupLogging')
    81→    expect(contents).toContain('[INFO]')
    82→  })
    83→})
    84→
    85→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86→// getLogger
    87→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    88→
    89→describe('getLogger', () => {
    90→  it('returns the same instance as setupLogging', () => {
    91→    const logger1 = setupLogging('info')
    92→    const logger2 = getLogger()
    93→    // Both should be the same singleton
    94→    expect(logger2).toBe(logger1)
    95→  })
    96→
    97→  it('auto-creates a logger if setupLogging was not called', () => {
    98→    const logger = getLogger()
    99→    expect(typeof logger.info).toBe('function')
   100→    // Should still be able to write
   101→    logger.info('auto-created logger message')
   102→    const contents = readLogContents()
   103→    expect(contents).toContain('auto-created logger message')
   104→  })
   105→})
   106→
   107→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   108→// Log level filtering
   109→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→
   111→describe('log level filtering', () => {
   112→  it('filters debug messages when level is info', () => {
   113→    const logger = setupLogging('info')
   114→    logger.debug('this should be filtered')
   115→    logger.info('this should appear')
   116→
   117→    const contents = readLogContents()
   118→    expect(contents).not.toContain('this should be filtered')
   119→    expect(contents).toContain('this should appear')
   120→  })
   121→
   122→  it('includes debug messages when level is debug', () => {
   123→    const logger = setupLogging('debug')
   124→    logger.debug('debug message visible')
   125→
   126→    const contents = readLogContents()
   127→    expect(contents).toContain('debug message visible')
   128→    expect(contents).toContain('[DEBUG]')
   129→  })
   130→})
   131→
   132→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   133→// Log file path includes date
   134→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   135→
   136→describe('log file naming', () => {
   137→  it('log file name includes today date in YYYY-MM-DD format', () => {
   138→    setupLogging('info')
   139→    getLogger().info('trigger file creation')
   140→
   141→    const fileName = findLogFile()
   142→    expect(fileName).toBeTruthy()
   143→    // File should match heartbeat-YYYY-MM-DD.log
   144→    expect(fileName).toMatch(/^heartbeat-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.log$/)
   145→  })
   146→})
   147→

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Mock homedir so any internal path helpers (logging, alerts, config, lock)
     8→// all point to a temp directory and don't pollute the real home.
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→
    11→let tmpDir: string
    12→
    13→vi.mock('node:os', async () => {
    14→  const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('node:os')>('node:os')
    15→  return {
    16→    ...actual,
    17→    homedir: () => tmpDir,
    18→  }
    19→})
    20→
    21→import { scanSensitive } from '../../src/heartbeat/heartbeat.js'
    22→import { _resetLogger, setupLogging } from '../../src/heartbeat/log.js'
    23→import { readAlerts, clearAlerts } from '../../src/heartbeat/alert.js'
    24→import type { ParsedTranscript } from '../../src/types/transcript.js'
    25→
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→// Setup / Teardown
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→
    30→beforeEach(() => {
    31→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'hb-test-'))
    32→  _resetLogger()
    33→  setupLogging('warn') // suppress info/debug noise during tests
    34→})
    35→
    36→afterEach(() => {
    37→  clearAlerts()
    38→  _resetLogger()
    39→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    40→})
    41→
    42→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→// Helpers
    44→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→
    46→function makeTranscript(texts: string[]): ParsedTranscript {
    47→  return {
    48→    client: 'claude',
    49→    session_id: 'test-session',
    50→    title: 'Test',
    51→    started_at: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z',
    52→    cwd: '/tmp/project',
    53→    branch: 'main',
    54→    messages: texts.map(text => ({ role: 'user', text, timestamp: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z' })),
    55→    tool_events: [],
    56→    source_path: '/tmp/transcript.json',
    57→  }
    58→}
    59→
    60→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    61→// scanSensitive — detection
    62→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    63→
    64→describe('scanSensitive', () => {
    65→  it('detects API key pattern: api_key = sk-1234567890', () => {
    66→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['Set api_key = sk-1234567890abcdef'])
    67→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
    68→
    69→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    70→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    71→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
    72→  })
    73→
    74→  it('detects password pattern: password = secret123', () => {
    75→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['password = secret123'])
    76→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
    77→
    78→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    79→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    80→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
    81→  })
    82→
    83→  it('detects token pattern: token = abc123xyz', () => {
    84→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['secret = my-secret-value'])
    85→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
    86→
    87→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    88→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    89→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
    90→  })
    91→
    92→  it('detects known secret prefixes: sk-xxxx, ghp_xxxx', () => {
    93→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['Use sk-abcdef1234567890extra'])
    94→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
    95→
    96→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    97→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    98→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
    99→  })
   100→
   101→  it('detects GitHub PAT prefix: ghp_xxxx', () => {
   102→    clearAlerts()
   103→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['Token ghp_abcdef1234567890extraextra'])
   104→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   105→
   106→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   107→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   108→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  it('detects Bearer token pattern', () => {
   112→    clearAlerts()
   113→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.abcdefghij'])
   114→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   115→
   116→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   117→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   118→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
   119→  })
   120→
   121→  it('does not flag normal text without sensitive patterns', () => {
   122→    const parsed = makeTranscript([
   123→      'This is a normal conversation about code.',
   124→      'We discussed the API design for the project.',
   125→      'The function returns a list of tokens from the parser.',
   126→    ])
   127→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   128→
   129→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   130→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(0)
   131→  })
   132→
   133→  it('stops after first match (returns early)', () => {
   134→    const parsed = makeTranscript([
   135→      'api_key = first-key',
   136→      'password = second-secret',
   137→    ])
   138→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   139→
   140→    // Should produce exactly one alert (returns after first sensitive match)
   141→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   142→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   143→  })
   144→})
   145→
   146→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   147→// SENSITIVE_PATTERNS — individual regex verification
   148→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   149→
   150→describe('SENSITIVE_PATTERNS individual patterns', () => {
   151→  it('api_key pattern matches various forms', () => {
   152→    const apiKeyPattern = /(?:api[_-]?key|apikey)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i
   153→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('api_key = abc123')).toBe(true)
   154→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('api-key: abc123')).toBe(true)
   155→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('apikey=abc123')).toBe(true)
   156→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('APIKEY = ABC')).toBe(true)
   157→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('no match here')).toBe(false)
   158→  })
   159→
   160→  it('password pattern matches password/passwd/pwd', () => {
   161→    const pwdPattern = /(?:password|passwd|pwd)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i
   162→    expect(pwdPattern.test('password = secret')).toBe(true)
   163→    expect(pwdPattern.test('passwd: hidden')).toBe(true)
   164→    expect(pwdPattern.test('PWD=test123')).toBe(true)
   165→    expect(pwdPattern.test('no match')).toBe(false)
   166→  })
   167→
   168→  it('secret/token pattern', () => {
   169→    const secretPattern = /(?:secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i
   170→    expect(secretPattern.test('secret = mysecret')).toBe(true)
   171→    expect(secretPattern.test('TOKEN: mytoken')).toBe(true)
   172→    expect(secretPattern.test('no match')).toBe(false)
   173→  })
   174→
   175→  it('known prefix pattern matches sk-, ghp_, glpat-, etc.', () => {
   176→    const prefixPattern = /(?:sk-|pk_live_|sk_live_|ghp_|gho_|glpat-)\S{10,}/
   177→    expect(prefixPattern.test('sk-abcdefghij1234567890')).toBe(true)
   178→    expect(prefixPattern.test('ghp_1234567890abcdef')).toBe(true)
   179→    expect(prefixPattern.test('pk_live_xxxxxxxxxx')).toBe(true)
   180→    expect(prefixPattern.test('sk_live_1234567890')).toBe(true)
   181→    expect(prefixPattern.test('gho_1234567890abcdef')).toBe(true)
   182→    expect(prefixPattern.test('glpat-1234567890abcdef')).toBe(true)
   183→    expect(prefixPattern.test('sk-short')).toBe(false) // too short
   184→    expect(prefixPattern.test('normal text')).toBe(false)
   185→  })
   186→
   187→  it('Bearer token pattern matches long bearer strings', () => {
   188→    const bearerPattern = /Bearer\s+\S{20,}/i
   189→    expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9abc')).toBe(true)
   190→    expect(bearerPattern.test('bearer AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA')).toBe(true)
   191→    expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer short')).toBe(false)
   192→    expect(bearerPattern.test('no bearer here')).toBe(false)
   193→  })
   194→})
   195→

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, rmSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Mock homedir so lockPath() points to a temp directory
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→let tmpDir: string
    11→
    12→vi.mock('node:os', async () => {
    13→  const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('node:os')>('node:os')
    14→  return {
    15→    ...actual,
    16→    homedir: () => tmpDir,
    17→  }
    18→})
    19→
    20→import {
    21→  acquireLock,
    22→  releaseLock,
    23→  readLockPid,
    24→  isProcessAlive,
    25→  lockPath,
    26→} from '../../src/heartbeat/lock.js'
    27→
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→// Setup / Teardown
    30→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31→
    32→beforeEach(() => {
    33→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'lock-test-'))
    34→})
    35→
    36→afterEach(() => {
    37→  // Always release before cleaning up to avoid leftover lock files
    38→  try {
    39→    releaseLock()
    40→  } catch {
    41→    // ignore
    42→  }
    43→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    44→})
    45→
    46→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    47→// lockPath
    48→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    49→
    50→describe('lockPath', () => {
    51→  it('returns a path inside <home>/.memorytree/', () => {
    52→    const path = lockPath()
    53→    expect(path).toContain('.memorytree')
    54→    expect(path).toContain('heartbeat.lock')
    55→    expect(path.startsWith(tmpDir)).toBe(true)
    56→  })
    57→})
    58→
    59→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→// acquireLock / releaseLock
    61→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→
    63→describe('acquireLock / releaseLock', () => {
    64→  it('acquireLock succeeds when no lock exists', () => {
    65→    expect(acquireLock()).toBe(true)
    66→  })
    67→
    68→  it('acquireLock fails when lock is held by current process', () => {
    69→    expect(acquireLock()).toBe(true)
    70→    // Second acquire should fail because current PID is alive
    71→    expect(acquireLock()).toBe(false)
    72→  })
    73→
    74→  it('releaseLock removes the lock file', () => {
    75→    acquireLock()
    76→    releaseLock()
    77→    expect(existsSync(lockPath())).toBe(false)
    78→  })
    79→
    80→  it('acquire succeeds after release', () => {
    81→    expect(acquireLock()).toBe(true)
    82→    releaseLock()
    83→    expect(acquireLock()).toBe(true)
    84→  })
    85→})
    86→
    87→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    88→// readLockPid
    89→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    90→
    91→describe('readLockPid', () => {
    92→  it('returns PID when lock is held', () => {
    93→    acquireLock()
    94→    const pid = readLockPid()
    95→    expect(pid).toBe(process.pid)
    96→  })
    97→
    98→  it('returns null when no lock file exists', () => {
    99→    expect(readLockPid()).toBeNull()
   100→  })
   101→
   102→  it('returns null for corrupt lock file', () => {
   103→    const path = lockPath()
   104→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })
   105→    writeFileSync(path, 'not-a-number')
   106→    expect(readLockPid()).toBeNull()
   107→  })
   108→})
   109→
   110→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   111→// isProcessAlive
   112→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   113→
   114→describe('isProcessAlive', () => {
   115→  it('returns true for current process PID', () => {
   116→    expect(isProcessAlive(process.pid)).toBe(true)
   117→  })
   118→
   119→  it('returns false for PID 0', () => {
   120→    expect(isProcessAlive(0)).toBe(false)
   121→  })
   122→
   123→  it('returns false for negative PID', () => {
   124→    expect(isProcessAlive(-1)).toBe(false)
   125→  })
   126→
   127→  it('returns false for very large PID (unlikely to exist)', () => {
   128→    expect(isProcessAlive(9999999)).toBe(false)
   129→  })
   130→})
   131→
   132→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   133→// Stale lock detection
   134→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   135→
   136→describe('stale lock detection', () => {
   137→  it('reclaims lock file with dead PID', () => {
   138→    const path = lockPath()
   139→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })
   140→    // Write a PID that is almost certainly not running
   141→    writeFileSync(path, '9999999')
   142→
   143→    // acquireLock should detect the dead PID and reclaim
   144→    expect(acquireLock()).toBe(true)
   145→
   146→    // Verify the lock now belongs to us
   147→    expect(readLockPid()).toBe(process.pid)
   148→  })
   149→
   150→  it('reclaims lock with corrupt content', () => {
   151→    const path = lockPath()
   152→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })
   153→    writeFileSync(path, 'garbage-content')
   154→
   155→    expect(acquireLock()).toBe(true)
   156→    expect(readLockPid()).toBe(process.pid)
   157→  })
   158→})
   159→

1→import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
     2→

    73→
    74→  it('shows lock held when PID exists', async () => {

    78→    vi.doMock('../../src/heartbeat/config.js', () => ({
    79→      configPath: () => '/nonexistent/config.toml',
    80→      loadConfig: () => ({
    81→        heartbeat_interval: '5m',
    82→        auto_push: true,
    83→        projects: [],
    84→        watch_dirs: [],
    85→        log_level: 'info',
    86→      }),
    87→      intervalToSeconds: () => 300,
    88→      saveConfig: () => {},
    89→    }))
    90→
    91→    const mod = await import('../../src/cli/cmd-daemon.js')
    92→    const result = mod.cmdStatus()
    93→    expect(result).toBe(0)
    94→
    95→    const output = stdoutChunks.join('')
    96→    expect(output).toContain('held by PID 12345')
    97→  })
    98→})
    99→
   100→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   101→// Platform detection helpers
   102→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   103→
   104→describe('isCronRegistered', () => {
   105→  afterEach(() => {
   106→    vi.restoreAllMocks()
   107→    vi.resetModules()
   108→  })
   109→
   110→  it('returns false when crontab output is empty', async () => {

   128→
   129→describe('isLaunchdRegistered', () => {
   130→  it('returns false when plist file does not exist', () => {

   132→    // In test env, the plist will not exist

   193→
   194→    const mod = await import('../../src/cli/cmd-daemon.js')
   195→    mod.cmdInstall({ interval: '10m', autoPush: 'false' })
   196→
   197→    expect(savedConfigs.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)
   198→    const saved = savedConfigs[0] as Record<string, unknown>
   199→    expect(saved['heartbeat_interval']).toBe('10m')

   204→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join, resolve } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→import {
     7→  cwdMatches,
     8→  findLatestFromJsonl,
     9→  formatText,
    10→  type RecallResult,
    11→} from '../../src/recall/recall.js'
    12→import { toPosixPath } from '../../src/utils/path.js'
    13→
    14→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→// cwdMatches
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→describe('cwdMatches', () => {
    19→  it('returns true for exact match', () => {
    20→    const root = toPosixPath(resolve('/projects/my-app')).toLowerCase()
    21→    expect(cwdMatches('/projects/my-app', root)).toBe(true)
    22→  })
    23→
    24→  it('returns true for subdirectory match', () => {
    25→    const root = toPosixPath(resolve('/projects/my-app')).toLowerCase()
    26→    expect(cwdMatches('/projects/my-app/src/utils', root)).toBe(true)
    27→  })
    28→
    29→  it('returns false when cwd does not match', () => {
    30→    const root = toPosixPath(resolve('/projects/my-app')).toLowerCase()
    31→    expect(cwdMatches('/other/project', root)).toBe(false)
    32→  })
    33→
    34→  it('returns false for empty cwd', () => {
    35→    const root = toPosixPath(resolve('/projects/my-app')).toLowerCase()
    36→    expect(cwdMatches('', root)).toBe(false)
    37→  })
    38→})
    39→
    40→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→// findLatestFromJsonl
    42→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→
    44→describe('findLatestFromJsonl', () => {
    45→  let tmpDir: string
    46→
    47→  beforeEach(() => {
    48→    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'recall-jsonl-'))
    49→  })
    50→  afterEach(() => {
    51→    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    52→  })
    53→
    54→  it('finds the latest session matching the project slug', () => {
    55→    const indexDir = join(tmpDir, 'index')
    56→    mkdirSync(indexDir, { recursive: true })
    57→
    58→    const sessions = [
    59→      { project: 'my-app', started_at: '2024-01-01T10:00:00Z', session_id: 'older' },
    60→      { project: 'my-app', started_at: '2024-06-15T10:00:00Z', session_id: 'newer' },
    61→    ]
    62→    writeFileSync(
    63→      join(indexDir, 'sessions.jsonl'),
    64→      sessions.map(s => JSON.stringify(s)).join('\n') + '\n',
    65→    )
    66→
    67→    const result = findLatestFromJsonl(tmpDir, '/some/path', 'my-app', '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z')
    68→    expect(result).not.toBeNull()
    69→    expect(result!['session_id']).toBe('newer')
    70→  })
    71→
    72→  it('filters out sessions at or after activation time', () => {
    73→    const indexDir = join(tmpDir, 'index')
    74→    mkdirSync(indexDir, { recursive: true })
    75→
    76→    const sessions = [
    77→      { project: 'my-app', started_at: '2024-01-01T10:00:00Z', session_id: 'before' },
    78→      { project: 'my-app', started_at: '2025-06-01T00:00:00Z', session_id: 'after' },
    79→    ]
    80→    writeFileSync(
    81→      join(indexDir, 'sessions.jsonl'),
    82→      sessions.map(s => JSON.stringify(s)).join('\n') + '\n',
    83→    )
    84→
    85→    const result = findLatestFromJsonl(tmpDir, '/some/path', 'my-app', '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z')
    86→    expect(result).not.toBeNull()
    87→    expect(result!['session_id']).toBe('before')
    88→  })
    89→
    90→  it('returns null when no sessions match the project', () => {
    91→    const indexDir = join(tmpDir, 'index')
    92→    mkdirSync(indexDir, { recursive: true })
    93→
    94→    const sessions = [
    95→      { project: 'other-project', started_at: '2024-01-01T10:00:00Z', session_id: 's1' },
    96→    ]
    97→    writeFileSync(
    98→      join(indexDir, 'sessions.jsonl'),
    99→      sessions.map(s => JSON.stringify(s)).join('\n') + '\n',
   100→    )
   101→
   102→    const result = findLatestFromJsonl(tmpDir, '/some/path', 'my-app', '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z')
   103→    expect(result).toBeNull()
   104→  })
   105→
   106→  it('returns null when sessions.jsonl does not exist', () => {
   107→    const result = findLatestFromJsonl(tmpDir, '/some/path', 'my-app', '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z')
   108→    expect(result).toBeNull()
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  it('matches sessions by cwd in addition to project slug', () => {
   112→    const indexDir = join(tmpDir, 'index')
   113→    mkdirSync(indexDir, { recursive: true })
   114→
   115→    // Use a real temp path so resolve() works correctly
   116→    const projectRoot = join(tmpDir, 'projects', 'my-app')
   117→    mkdirSync(projectRoot, { recursive: true })
   118→    const resolvedRoot = toPosixPath(resolve(projectRoot))
   119→
   120→    const sessions = [
   121→      { project: 'unrelated', cwd: resolvedRoot, started_at: '2024-01-01T10:00:00Z', session_id: 'cwd-match' },
   122→    ]
   123→    writeFileSync(
   124→      join(indexDir, 'sessions.jsonl'),
   125→      sessions.map(s => JSON.stringify(s)).join('\n') + '\n',
   126→    )
   127→
   128→    const result = findLatestFromJsonl(tmpDir, projectRoot, 'different-slug', '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z')
   129→    expect(result).not.toBeNull()
   130→    expect(result!['session_id']).toBe('cwd-match')
   131→  })
   132→
   133→  it('skips malformed JSON lines', () => {
   134→    const indexDir = join(tmpDir, 'index')
   135→    mkdirSync(indexDir, { recursive: true })
   136→
   137→    const content = [
   138→      'not valid json',
   139→      JSON.stringify({ project: 'my-app', started_at: '2024-01-01T10:00:00Z', session_id: 'valid' }),
   140→      '{ broken: }',
   141→    ].join('\n') + '\n'
   142→    writeFileSync(join(indexDir, 'sessions.jsonl'), content)
   143→
   144→    const result = findLatestFromJsonl(tmpDir, '/some/path', 'my-app', '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z')
   145→    expect(result).not.toBeNull()
   146→    expect(result!['session_id']).toBe('valid')
   147→  })
   148→})
   149→
   150→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   151→// formatText
   152→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   153→
   154→describe('formatText', () => {
   155→  it('formats a found result with session details', () => {
   156→    const payload: RecallResult = {
   157→      found: true,
   158→      project: 'my-app',
   159→      repo: '/projects/my-app',
   160→      imported_count: 3,
   161→      client: 'claude',
   162→      session_id: 'abc-123',
   163→      title: 'Test Session',
   164→      started_at: '2024-06-15T10:00:00Z',
   165→      cwd: '/projects/my-app',
   166→      branch: 'main',
   167→      message_count: 42,
   168→      tool_event_count: 7,
   169→      global_clean_path: '/tmp/clean.md',
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    const text = formatText(payload)
   173→    expect(text).toContain('project: my-app')
   174→    expect(text).toContain('client: claude')
   175→    expect(text).toContain('session_id: abc-123')
   176→    expect(text).toContain('title: Test Session')
   177→    expect(text).toContain('started_at: 2024-06-15T10:00:00Z')
   178→    expect(text).toContain('branch: main')

   180→    expect(text).toContain('tool_events: 7')
   181→    expect(text).toContain('imported_this_sync: 3')

   183→
   184→  it('formats a not-found result with message', () => {

   187→      project: 'my-app',

   206→      global_clean_path: '/tmp/clean.md',
   207→    }
   208→
   209→    const text = formatText(payload)
   210→    expect(text).toContain('--- clean transcript content ---')
   211→    expect(text).toContain('This is the transcript content.')
   212→  })
   213→})

    39→describe('buildDatetime', () => {

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'

    18→beforeEach(() => {
    19→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'locale-test-'))
    20→})
    21→
    22→afterEach(() => {
    23→  vi.unstubAllEnvs()
    24→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })

    26→
    27→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→// normalizeLocale
    29→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→
    31→describe('normalizeLocale', () => {
    32→  it('passes "en" through as "en"', () => {
    33→    expect(normalizeLocale('en')).toBe('en')
    34→  })
    35→
    36→  it('passes "zh-cn" through as "zh-cn"', () => {
    37→    expect(normalizeLocale('zh-cn')).toBe('zh-cn')
    38→  })
    39→
    40→  it('maps "zh" to "zh-cn"', () => {
    41→    expect(normalizeLocale('zh')).toBe('zh-cn')
    42→  })
    43→
    44→  it('maps "en-us" to "en"', () => {
    45→    expect(normalizeLocale('en-us')).toBe('en')
    46→  })
    47→
    48→  it('maps "zh-tw" to "zh-cn"', () => {
    49→    expect(normalizeLocale('zh-tw')).toBe('zh-cn')
    50→  })
    51→
    52→  it('detects locale from repo root when value is "auto"', () => {
    53→    // Create a repo with English README
    54→    writeFileSync(
    55→      join(tmpDir, 'README.md'),
    56→      'This is a project with enough English words to trigger detection. ' +
    57→      'The project provides utilities for text processing and data analysis. ' +
    58→      'It includes modules for parsing, indexing, and searching content.',
    59→    )
    60→    const result = normalizeLocale('auto', tmpDir)
    61→    expect(result).toBe('en')
    62→  })

    64→  it('passes through unknown locale values unchanged', () => {
    65→    expect(normalizeLocale('fr')).toBe('fr')
    66→    expect(normalizeLocale('ja')).toBe('ja')
    67→    expect(normalizeLocale('de')).toBe('de')
    68→  })
    69→})

    81→      'It includes modules for parsing structured documents, building search indexes, ' +

    83→      'Additional features include automated testing, continuous integration support, ' +

    92→    writeFileSync(
    93→      join(tmpDir, 'README.md'),
    94→      '这是一个关于文本处理和数据分析的综合项目。' +
    95→      '本项目提供了一套实用工具集，用于解析结构化文档、建立搜索索引和执行全文搜索。' +

    98→    )
    99→    expect(detectRepoLocale(tmpDir)).toBe('zh-cn')
   100→  })
   101→
   102→  it('returns null for mixed repo with balanced content', () => {

   104→    writeFileSync(

   111→      '功能包括搜索、去重和自动化报告。',
   112→    )
   113→    const result = detectRepoLocale(tmpDir)
   114→    // With balanced content, either null or one of the two is acceptable
   115→    expect([null, 'en', 'zh-cn']).toContain(result)
   116→  })
   117→
   118→  it('returns null for empty repo with no text files', () => {
   119→    expect(detectRepoLocale(tmpDir)).toBeNull()
   120→  })

   122→  it('returns null for non-existent directory', () => {

   139→describe('detectSystemLocale', () => {
   140→  it('returns "zh-cn" when LC_ALL is zh_CN', () => {
   141→    vi.stubEnv('LC_ALL', 'zh_CN.UTF-8')
   142→    vi.stubEnv('LANG', '')
   143→    vi.stubEnv('LANGUAGE', '')
   144→    expect(detectSystemLocale()).toBe('zh-cn')
   145→  })
   146→

   149→    vi.stubEnv('LANG', 'en_US.UTF-8')
   150→    vi.stubEnv('LANGUAGE', '')
   151→    expect(detectSystemLocale()).toBe('en')
   152→  })
   153→
   154→  it('returns "en" when no locale env vars are set', () => {
   155→    vi.stubEnv('LC_ALL', '')
   156→    vi.stubEnv('LANG', '')
   157→    vi.stubEnv('LANGUAGE', '')
   158→    expect(detectSystemLocale()).toBe('en')
   159→  })
   160→
   161→  it('returns "zh-cn" when LANGUAGE contains "chinese"', () => {

   163→    vi.stubEnv('LANG', '')
   164→    vi.stubEnv('LANGUAGE', 'Chinese')

   167→
   168→  it('prioritizes LC_ALL over LANG', () => {

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'

     5→
     6→import {
     7→  detectState,

    24→
    25→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    28→
    29→let tmpDir: string
    30→
    31→beforeEach(() => {

    38→
    39→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    43→describe('detectState', () => {

    52→
    53→  it('returns "installed" for full setup with complete AGENTS.md', () => {
    54→    createMemoryDirs(tmpDir)
    55→
    56→    const goalDir = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '01_goals')
    57→    const todoDir = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '02_todos')

    61→    writeFileSync(join(chatDir, '2025-06-15_14-30.md'), '# Chat')
    62→
    63→    // Write a complete AGENTS.md with all 9 signals

    65→

    68→

    75→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    79→describe('extractGoalVersion', () => {

    83→
    84→  it('extracts version from Windows-style path', () => {
    85→    expect(extractGoalVersion('C:\\path\\to\\goal_v005_20250615.md')).toBe('005')
    86→  })
    87→
    88→  it('returns null for non-matching filename', () => {
    89→    expect(extractGoalVersion('/path/to/readme.md')).toBeNull()
    90→  })
    91→
    92→  it('returns null for null input', () => {
    93→    expect(extractGoalVersion(null)).toBeNull()
    94→  })
    95→})

    97→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    98→// needsAgentsMerge
    99→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   100→
   101→describe('needsAgentsMerge', () => {
   102→  it('returns true for incomplete AGENTS.md', () => {
   103→    const agentsPath = join(tmpDir, 'AGENTS.md')
   104→    writeFileSync(agentsPath, '# AGENTS\nSome basic instructions.\n')
   105→    expect(needsAgentsMerge(agentsPath)).toBe(true)

   107→
   108→  it('returns false for complete AGENTS.md with all signals', () => {

   168→describe('hasRepoSafetyRule', () => {

   232→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   233→// upgrade (end-to-end)
   234→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   235→
   236→describe('upgrade', () => {
   237→  it('performs full upgrade on empty directory', () => {

     4→describe('toPosixPath', () => {
     5→  it('converts Windows backslashes to forward slashes', () => {

    18→    expect(toPosixPath('')).toBe('.')

    20→
    21→  it('normalizes dot segments', () => {
    22→    expect(toPosixPath('a/b/../c')).toBe('a/c')
